description_long: This object is a vintage corded telephone, reminiscent of early 20th-century designs, featuring a prominent rotary mechanism and ornate metallic detailing. The base is robust and decorated with intricate patterns, supporting a central column that rises to hold the receiver cradle. The receiver itself is cylindrical with metallic end caps and is connected to the base by a thick, coiled cord. A separate earpiece and mouthpiece are visible, both attached to the main body by metal arms and hooks. The rotary dial is mounted on the side, and a secondary cord leads to a round, buttoned component, likely for line connection or switching. The overall color scheme is dominated by black and brass tones, with the cords appearing as braided fabric.
description: A vintage, ornate corded telephone with a rotary dial, metallic accents, and a coiled cord connecting the cylindrical receiver to the decorated base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the ornate base, rotary dial, and the receiver resting on its cradle with both cords visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the rotary dial mechanism and the connection point for the secondary cord.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the base, the coiled cord, and the round buttoned component lying on the surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the coiled cord and the alignment of the receiver and cradle.
